zotero.abstractNote: We study the consistency of kernel interpolation in Sobolev spaces Hˆs(Rˆd) with s d/2. We show that for d=1 and s=1, the interpolant of n i.i.d. samples does not converge to the true function in the Lˆ2-norm as n goes to infinity, even if the true function is smooth. This result is extended to d=2 and s=1, and to higher dimensions for s close to d/2. Our results imply that kernel interpolation is not universally consistent in these spaces, which stands in contrast to the case of high-dimensional Sobolev spaces where consistency has been established.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of 35th Conference on Learning Theory
